locked_parameters.P: P is the observed law of (Y,D,X) from a randomized or unconfounded binary-treatment design, with continuously distributed potential-outcome marginals F_1(\cdot\mid X=x) and F_0(\cdot\mid X=x) identified from P.
locked_parameters.theta: \theta is the unconditional probability of benefit \theta=\Pr(Y_1>Y_0)=\E\{\Pr(Y_1>Y_0\mid X)\}.
locked_parameters.R_rho: \mathcal R_{\rho}=[\rho_-,\rho_+]\subset[-1,1] is the locked Spearman copula band.
locked_parameters.B_rho(P): \mathcal B_{\rho}(P)=[L_{\rho}(P),U_{\rho}(P)] is the outer envelope obtained by optimizing \int \one\{Q_1(u\mid x)>Q_0(v\mid x)\}\,dC_x(u,v) over all copulas C_x with uniform margins and \rho_S(C_x)\in[\rho_-,\rho_+], then integrating over X. No parametric copula family, ordinal threshold model, or regression specification is locked.

named_assumptions.label: ass:sampling
named_assumptions.statement: The observed data are i.i.d. draws of O=(Y,D,X), the consistency relation Y=DY_1+(1-D)Y_0 holds almost surely, and SUTVA rules out interference and hidden treatment versions.

named_assumptions.label: ass:unconf
named_assumptions.statement: (Y_1,Y_0) \perp D\mid X and 0<\Pr(D=1\mid X=x)<1 for P_X-almost every x. Hence the conditional distributions of Y_1 and Y_0 are identified from the observed treatment and control subsamples within every relevant covariate stratum.

named_assumptions.label: ass:continuous
named_assumptions.statement: For P_X-almost every x, the conditional marginal distribution functions F_1(\cdot\mid x) and F_0(\cdot\mid x) are continuous and strictly increasing on compact supports, and their densities are bounded above and away from zero on those supports.

named_assumptions.label: ass:band
named_assumptions.statement: For P_X-almost every x, the conditional copula measure \gamma_x of (Y_1,Y_0)\mid X=x has uniform margins and satisfies \rho_S(\gamma_x)\in[\rho_-,\rho_+], where -1<\rho_-\le\rho_+<1.

named_assumptions.label: ass:regular
named_assumptions.statement: Let \eta(P) denote the finite collection of cell probabilities and conditional quantile functions that indexes the endpoint map in the finite-support regime. For each endpoint under study, the population value map \Phi_\rho:\eta\mapsto(L_\rho(\eta),U_\rho(\eta)) is finite at \eta(P), directionally Hadamard differentiable there tangentially to the finite-cell empirical quantile process, and admits a nonempty compact set of normalized dual optimizers at every P_X-relevant covariate value. This assumption is recorded only for later endpoint-law work; it does not by itself assert sharpness of the outer envelope, endpoint-attaining measurable selectors, or an exact no-gap dual formula for the continuous strict-indicator problem.

named_assumptions.label: ass:stable-dual
named_assumptions.statement: There exists a deterministic neighborhood \mathcal N of \eta(P) whose radius dominates the bootstrap scale m_n^{-1/2} such that, for every \eta\in\mathcal N, the binding status of the lower and upper Spearman moment inequalities and the collection of active dual inequalities are constant. Moreover, if \eta_n,\tilde\eta_n\in\mathcal N and h_n\to h in the tangent space, then \dot\Phi_{\rho,\eta_n}(h_n)-\dot\Phi_{\rho,\tilde\eta_n}(h)\to0. This is the uniform continuity needed to replace the shifted derivative along the bootstrap path by the derivative at the population face.

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite-cell
named_assumptions.statement: For the sieve-consistency supporting theorem and the later bootstrap conjecture, X has finite support \mathcal X=\{x_1,\ldots,x_J\}, each cell has strictly positive mass, and treatment propensities in every cell are bounded away from zero and one. Writing u_\ell=\ell/(K_n+1), the cellwise empirical quantiles obey \max_{d\in\{0,1\}}\max_{1\le j\le J}\max_{1\le \ell\le K_n}|\widehat Q_d(u_\ell\mid x_j)-Q_d(u_\ell\mid x_j)|=o_p(1) with \log K_n/n\to0, and the bootstrap-side grid condition \log K_n/m_n\to0 also holds. For each cell x_j, each grid size K, and each \delta>0, define B_{j,K}(\delta;P)=\{(\ell,m):|Q_1(u_\ell\mid x_j)-Q_0(u_m\mid x_j)|\le \delta\}. There exists a fixed deterministic neighborhood \mathcal N_{\mathrm{cb}} of \eta(P) and a deterministic modulus \omega(\delta)\downarrow0 such that, uniformly over j, over all large K, and over every optimizer or r_K-near-optimizer of the lower or upper checkerboard program built either from the population quantile array or from any quantile array indexed by \eta\in\mathcal N_{\mathrm{cb}}, we have \sum_{(\ell,m)\in B_{j,K}(\delta;P)}\pi_{\ell m}\le \omega(\delta) with r_K\downarrow0. Finally, the deterministic checkerboard approximation bias satisfies the pointwise bound |L_{\rho,K}(P)-L_\rho(P)|+|U_{\rho,K}(P)-U_\rho(P)|=b_K\to0, and for the bootstrap conjecture the checkerboard approximation is locally uniform on \mathcal N_{\mathrm{cb}}: \sup_{\eta\in\mathcal N_{\mathrm{cb}}}\|\Phi_{\rho,K_n}(\eta)-\Phi_\rho(\eta)\|=b_{K_n}^{\mathrm{loc}}=o(m_n^{-1/2}). These conditions suffice for the supporting consistency theorem and for bootstrap-scale checkerboard control. They do not, by themselves, lock in the stronger root-n local sieve remainder that a full proof of Conjecture 2 may require, nor do they replace the separate stable-face assumption used to control the directional derivative on the bootstrap path.

conjectures.label: sharp-dual
conjectures.statement:
(NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:band}, the sharp identified set for \(\theta=\Pr(Y_1>Y_0)\) is exactly the closed interval \([L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P)]\).  For each \(x\), the conditional value set \(I_x=\{\int h_x\,d\gamma:\gamma\in\Gamma_x(\rho_-,\rho_+)\}\) is the closed interval \([L_x(P),U_x(P)]\), the lower endpoint is attained by a measurable copula selector \(x\mapsto\gamma_x^L\) and equals
\[
\sup_{\alpha,\beta,\lambda_-,\lambda_+}
\left\{\int_0^1\alpha(u)\,du+\int_0^1\beta(v)\,dv+\lambda_-a_- - \lambda_+a_+\right\},
\]
where \(\lambda_-,\lambda_+\ge0\) and
\[
\alpha(u)+\beta(v)\le h_x(u,v)+(\lambda_+-\lambda_-)uv\quad\text{for all }(u,v)\in[0,1]^2,
\]
and the upper endpoint is attained by a measurable selector \(x\mapsto\gamma_x^U\) and is obtained by applying the same formula to \(-h_x\) and changing sign.

method_exemplar.carried_over: Use transport duality with marginal constraints and the additional Spearman moment band to express each endpoint as a computable dual value.

conjectures.label: inference
conjectures.statement: (NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:regular}, \ref{ass:stable-dual}, and \ref{ass:finite-cell}, if \(b_{K_n}=o(n^{-1/2})\), \(\log K_n/n\to0\), and \(m_n\to\infty\) with \(m_n/n\to0\), then \(\sqrt n(\widehat L_\rho-L_\rho(P),\widehat U_\rho-U_\rho(P))\) converges to a directional endpoint limit law and the \(m_n\)-out-of-\(n\) bootstrap estimates it consistently.

method_exemplar.carried_over: Treat endpoint limit theory as the input to interval-width-aware confidence interval calibration for a partially identified scalar target.

supporting_theorems.label: thm:critical-set
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:band}, every feasible latent law consistent with a band \(R=[\rho_-,\rho_+]\) obeys \(L_R(P)\le \theta\le U_R(P)\). Consequently, the data and band imply a strict benefit majority whenever \(L_R(P)>1/2\), and imply a strict nonmajority whenever \(U_R(P)<1/2\). If \(L_R(P)\le 1/2\le U_R(P)\), then the outer envelope alone does not certify either strict conclusion; the converse ``if and only if'' claim is deferred to the sharpness conjecture.
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: outer-envelope certification of the majority sign]\label{thm:critical-set}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:band}, every feasible latent law consistent with a band \(R=[\rho_-,\rho_+]\) obeys
\[
L_R(P)\le \theta\le U_R(P).
\]
Consequently, the data and band imply a strict benefit majority whenever \(L_R(P)>1/2\), and imply a strict nonmajority whenever \(U_R(P)<1/2\). If \(L_R(P)\le 1/2\le U_R(P)\), then the outer envelope alone does not certify either strict conclusion; the converse ``if and only if'' claim is deferred to the sharpness conjecture.
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Fix a closed band \(R=[\rho_-,\rho_+]\subset[-1,1]\). For any feasible latent law there is a measurable selection \(x\mapsto \gamma_x\) with \(\gamma_x\in\Gamma_x(R)\) for \(P_X\)-almost every \(x\). Its implied target value is
\[
\theta(\gamma_\cdot)=\int \left[\int_{[0,1]^2} h_x(u,v)\,d\gamma_x(u,v)\right] dP_X(x).
\]
By definition of the conditional band endpoints,
\[
L_{x,R}(P)\le \int h_x\,d\gamma_x\le U_{x,R}(P)
\qquad\text{for }P_X\text{-almost every }x.
\]
Integrating over \(P_X\) yields
\[
L_R(P)=\int L_{x,R}(P)\,dP_X(x)\le \theta(\gamma_\cdot)\le \int U_{x,R}(P)\,dP_X(x)=U_R(P).
\]
Therefore every feasible value of \(\theta\) lies in the outer envelope \([L_R(P),U_R(P)]\).

If \(L_R(P)>1/2\), then every feasible \(\theta(\gamma_\cdot)\) exceeds \(1/2\), so the data and band certify a strict benefit majority. If \(U_R(P)<1/2\), then every feasible \(\theta(\gamma_\cdot)\) is below \(1/2\), so the data and band certify a strict nonmajority. Finally, if \(L_R(P)\le 1/2\le U_R(P)\), then the outer envelope straddles the majority threshold. Without the sharpness statement conjectured for \([L_R(P),U_R(P)]\), this straddling rules out neither strict conclusion nor its opposite; it only says that the outer bound itself does not certify either one.\end{proof}

supporting_theorems.label: thm:sieve-consistency
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:regular} and \ref{ass:finite-cell}, if \(K_n\to\infty\), then \((\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho)\to_p(L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P))\).
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 2: sieve consistency of endpoint estimators]\label{thm:sieve-consistency}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:regular} and \ref{ass:finite-cell}, if \(K_n\to\infty\), then \((\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho)\to_p(L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P))\).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
For each cell \(x_j\) and grid size \(K\), let \(L_{j,K}(P)\) and \(U_{j,K}(P)\) be the deterministic checkerboard endpoint values built from the true cellwise quantiles, and let \(\widehat L_{j,K}\) and \(\widehat U_{j,K}\) be the corresponding values built from the empirical cellwise quantiles. Their common feasible set is the compact polytope
\[
\Pi_K(a_-,a_+)=\left\{\pi\in\mathbb R_+^{K\times K}:\sum_m\pi_{\ell m}=1/K,\ \sum_\ell\pi_{\ell m}=1/K,\ a_-\le \sum_{\ell,m}u_\ell u_m\pi_{\ell m}\le a_+\right\}.
\]
Write
\[
\Delta_{n,j,K}=\max_{d\in\{0,1\}}\max_{1\le \ell\le K}\bigl|\widehat Q_d(u_\ell\mid x_j)-Q_d(u_\ell\mid x_j)\bigr|.
\]
Assumption~\ref{ass:finite-cell} gives \(\max_{j\le J}\Delta_{n,j,K_n}=o_p(1)\). If
\[
\widehat h_{j,\ell m}=\one\{\widehat Q_1(u_\ell\mid x_j)>\widehat Q_0(u_m\mid x_j)\}
\quad\text{and}\quad
h_{j,\ell m}=\one\{Q_1(u_\ell\mid x_j)>Q_0(u_m\mid x_j)\},
\]
then \(\widehat h_{j,\ell m}\neq h_{j,\ell m}\) implies \((\ell,m)\in B_{j,K}(2\Delta_{n,j,K};P)\). Let \(\pi^{L}_{j,K}\) be an optimizer for the population lower program and \(\widehat\pi^{L}_{j,K}\) an optimizer for the empirical lower program. On the event that the empirical quantile array lies in the fixed neighborhood \(\mathcal N_{\mathrm{cb}}\) from Assumption~\ref{ass:finite-cell}, the near-boundary mass-control part of Assumption~\ref{ass:finite-cell} yields
\[
\sum_{(\ell,m)\in B_{j,K}(2\Delta_{n,j,K};P)} \pi^{L}_{j,K,\ell m}\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}),
\qquad
\sum_{(\ell,m)\in B_{j,K}(2\Delta_{n,j,K};P)} \widehat\pi^{L}_{j,K,\ell m}\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}).
\]
Using the optimality of \(\pi^{L}_{j,K}\) for the population objective and of \(\widehat\pi^{L}_{j,K}\) for the empirical objective,
\[
\widehat L_{j,K}-L_{j,K}(P)
\le \sum_{\ell,m}(\widehat h_{j,\ell m}-h_{j,\ell m})\pi^{L}_{j,K,\ell m}
\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}),
\]
and likewise
\[
L_{j,K}(P)-\widehat L_{j,K}
\le \sum_{\ell,m}(h_{j,\ell m}-\widehat h_{j,\ell m})\widehat\pi^{L}_{j,K,\ell m}
\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}).
\]
Hence
\[
\bigl|\widehat L_{j,K}-L_{j,K}(P)\bigr|\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}).
\]
The same argument with maximizers gives
\[
\bigl|\widehat U_{j,K}-U_{j,K}(P)\bigr|\le \omega(2\Delta_{n,j,K}).
\]
Because \(\omega(\delta)\to0\) as \(\delta\downarrow0\),
\[
\max_{j\le J}\bigl|\widehat L_{j,K_n}-L_{j,K_n}(P)\bigr|=o_p(1),
\qquad
\max_{j\le J}\bigl|\widehat U_{j,K_n}-U_{j,K_n}(P)\bigr|=o_p(1).
\]
Now let \(p_j=\Pr(X=x_j)\) and \(\widehat p_j=n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n\one\{X_i=x_j\}\). Since \(J<\infty\), the law of large numbers gives \(\max_{j\le J}|\widehat p_j-p_j|\to_p0\), and each endpoint lies in \([0,1]\). Therefore
\[
\widehat L_\rho-L_{\rho,K_n}(P)
=\sum_{j=1}^J(\widehat p_j-p_j)\widehat L_{j,K_n}+\sum_{j=1}^J p_j\bigl(\widehat L_{j,K_n}-L_{j,K_n}(P)\bigr)=o_p(1),
\]
and similarly
\[
\widehat U_\rho-U_{\rho,K_n}(P)=o_p(1).
\]
Finally, Assumption~\ref{ass:finite-cell} states that the deterministic checkerboard bias satisfies
\[
\bigl|L_{\rho,K_n}(P)-L_\rho(P)\bigr|+\bigl|U_{\rho,K_n}(P)-U_\rho(P)\bigr|=b_{K_n}\to0.
\]
The triangle inequality therefore yields
\[
\widehat L_\rho\to_p L_\rho(P),
\qquad
\widehat U_\rho\to_p U_\rho(P).
\]
Hence \((\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho)\to_p(L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P))\).\end{proof}

substrate_survey.notes: No match returned from lean_local_search in this workspace.
substrate_survey.reason: The target note studies a continuous copula transport functional with a Spearman band; the named Manski IV system was not surfaced directly by the cwd-independent search.
Filename-only survey confirms that generic potential-outcome, Manski, Lee, Balke-Pearl, and interval-inference modules exist under ../AutoID without loading Lean source.

substrate_survey.resolved: No directly reusable theorem-level analogue surfaced; only generic probability moment primitives were returned.
substrate_survey.reason: The broader theorem search did not expose a direct constrained-copula transport abstraction, which supports bypassing exact reuse for the present note.

The available AutoID modules cover partial-identification primitives, random sets, classical Manski/Lee/Balke-Pearl structures, and interval inference, but not the continuous Spearman-band copula transport problem at this abstraction level.

review_repair_notes: Recast the shared functional as an outer envelope and removed any setup-level implication that sharpness or endpoint attainment is already established.
Recorded explicitly that the shared regularity assumptions do not themselves provide endpoint-attaining selectors or a no-gap dual formula for the strict-indicator cost.

Strengthened the finite-cell regime with explicit near-boundary mass control, a bootstrap grid condition, and local-uniform checkerboard approximation, while noting that no root-n local sieve remainder is locked at setup stage.
Weakened the critical-set supporting theorem to one-sided majority-sign certification that follows from outer bounds alone.
